body:
👋🏻 I'm using SHA1 still it seems:

```
$ git rev-parse --show-object-format
sha1
```

And I can't get `git_commit` to show anything 😞

I've asked Gemini for a workaround and adding the following snippet to my starship.toml config file seems to work.

```
[custom.git_sha256]
description = "Fallback for SHA256 repos where built-in git fails"
# 1. The Command: Get the branch name
command = "git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD" 

# 2. The Condition: Only run IF we are in a repo AND it is sha256
# OPTIMIZED: Runs only ONE git process. 
# If not in a repo, git fails -> grep fails -> module hidden.
# If in SHA1 repo, git prints "sha1" -> grep fails -> module hidden.
# If in SHA256 repo, git prints "sha256" -> grep finds it -> module shown.
when = "git rev-parse --show-object-format 2>/dev/null | grep -q sha256"

# 3. Formatting
shell = ["bash", "--noprofile", "--norc"]
format = "on [$symbol$output]($style) "
style = "purple bold"
symbol = " "
```

I guess the `git` commands on the "when" section will make my shell slower when browsing folders but it seems to work fine.

> I've asked Gemini for a workaround and adding the following snippet to my starship.toml config file seems to work.
> 
> ```
> [custom.git_sha256]
> description = "Fallback for SHA256 repos where built-in git fails"
> # 1. The Command: Get the branch name
> command = "git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD" 
> 
> # 2. The Condition: Only run IF we are in a repo AND it is sha256
> # OPTIMIZED: Runs only ONE git process. 
> # If not in a repo, git fails -> grep fails -> module hidden.
> # If in SHA1 repo, git prints "sha1" -> grep fails -> module hidden.
> # If in SHA256 repo, git prints "sha256" -> grep finds it -> module shown.
> when = "git rev-parse --show-object-format 2>/dev/null | grep -q sha256"
> 
> # 3. Formatting
> shell = ["bash", "--noprofile", "--norc"]
> format = "on [$symbol$output]($style) "
> style = "purple bold"
> symbol = " "
> ```
> 
> I guess the `git` commands on the "when" section will make my shell slower when browsing folders but it seems to work fine.

Thanks this is a decent workaround for now

> > I've asked Gemini for a workaround and adding the following snippet to my starship.toml config file seems to work.
> > 
> > ```
> > [custom.git_sha256]
> > description = "Fallback for SHA256 repos where built-in git fails"
> > # 1. The Command: Get the branch name
> > command = "git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD" 
> > 
> > # 2. The Condition: Only run IF we are in a repo AND it is sha256
> > # OPTIMIZED: Runs only ONE git process. 
> > # If not in a repo, git fails -> grep fails -> module hidden.
> > # If in SHA1 repo, git prints "sha1" -> grep fails -> module hidden.
> > # If in SHA256 repo, git prints "sha256" -> grep finds it -> module shown.
> > when = "git rev-parse --show-object-format 2>/dev/null | grep -q sha256"
> > 
> > # 3. Formatting
> > shell = ["bash", "--noprofile", "--norc"]
> > format = "on [$symbol$output]($style) "
> > style = "purple bold"
> > symbol = " "
> > ```
> > 
> > I guess the `git` commands on the "when" section will make my shell slower when browsing folders but it seems to work fine.
> 
> Thanks this is a decent workaround for now

BTW, you can use a directories directive to only run them on specific paths

Edit: In fact, gemini hallucinated with the directories directive :D This is a best approach (ab)using PWD:

```
[custom.git_sha256]
description = "Fallback for SHA256 repos in a specific directory"
# Get the branch name
command = "git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD"

# THE LOGIC:
# 1. Check if PWD starts with your target path.
#    Note: The '*' at the end matches all subdirectories.
# 2. If yes, check if it's a git repo.
# 3. If yes, check if it's sha256.
when = """
target="/Users/name/projects/my-sha256-repo"; \
[[ "$PWD" == "$target"* ]] && \
git rev-parse --show-object-format 2>/dev/null | grep -q sha256
"""

# Shell settings (required for the [[ ]] syntax)
shell = ["bash", "--noprofile", "--norc"]

# Formatting (matches default Starship style)
format = "on [$symbol$output]($style) "
style = "purple bold"
symbol = " "
```

hey sry for disturbing.
i knew you are doing great works for keeping starship.rs nice.
but it would be good to know whether any progress is made for resolving this issues ?
